title: Stored XSS in Admin Role Edit Page via Unescaped Role Name
description: The Ultimate Member role edit admin page echoes the role name from get_option() without proper HTML escaping in two locations: (1) Line 247 outputs $data['name'] into an HTML input value attribute without esc_attr(); (2) Line 249 outputs $data['name'] with stripslashes() but without esc_html() in a <span> element. An administrator who saves a role with a malicious name containing HTML/JavaScript could achieve stored XSS affecting other administrators who view the role edit page.
impact: Admin-to-admin stored XSS. An attacker must already have manage_options capability (full administrator), making privilege escalation moot. The practical impact is limited to session hijacking between administrators or defacing the admin panel.
remediation: Apply esc_attr() at line 247 and esc_html() at line 249 when outputting the role name.
